what: Simulations of Lumpy Skin Disease Epidemics
title: Simulations of Lumpy Skin Disease Epidemics
text: Thibaut Jombart 2026-01-07 repository contains R package lsdsim implements stochastic, meta-population compartmental model Lumpy Skin Disease (LSD), including simulation different types interventions. supports paper titled “cull cull: model-based evaluation response strategies Lumpy Skin Disease outbreaks” Jombart Abbate (2025). paper currently peer-review, processed bioRxiv. Meanwhile, copy manuscript supporting material provided repository (folder paper). Direct links: manuscript supporting material repository also contains instance reportfactory run epidemic simulations reproducible way. repository still development. particular, documentation testing improved early 2026. intend using content, hesitate contact thibautjombart@gmail.com.

title: Simulations of Lumpy Skin Disease Epidemics
text: first need clone download content repository, open R session inside main folder. clone repository, use: already cloned repository, make sure latest version opening terminal main folder typing: use Rstudio, easiest way open session via opening file lsdv_simulations.Rproj. install lsdsim need devtools installed computer able compile R packages source. also need reportfactory package installed. latter handy handle dependencies Rmarkdown documents stored report_sources/.

title: Simulations of Lumpy Skin Disease Epidemics
text: following code install needed packages:
code: ## install and load lsdsim if (!require(devtools)) install.packages("devtools") devtools::load_all()  ## install reportfactory, install Rmd deps if (!require(reportfactory)) install.packages("reportfactory") reportfactory::install_deps()

title: Simulations of Lumpy Skin Disease Epidemics
text: Results presented initial publications stored : outputs/res_2025-12-29.qs: R object storing summaries 6,000 simulations outputs/simulations_final.html: report 6,000 simulations

title: Simulations of Lumpy Skin Disease Epidemics
text: reportfactory can used generate report running simulations epidemics different types responses, storing output time-stamped folder outputs/. sources report report_sources/simulations.Rmd. Users can modify report see fit, add new report report_sources folder run new simulations. simulations.Rmd report following parameters: n_rep: integer indicating number replicates generate; original study, used 1,000, beware take hours run standard desktop; defaults 10 run_sim: logical indicating whether new simulations run; FALSE, latest simulation results stored inside outputs used generate report; defaults TRUE instance, want generate new results 30 replicates, can type:
code: reportfactory::compile_reports(params = list(n_rep = 30, run_sim = TRUE)) >>> Compiling report: simulations       - with parameters: n_rep = 30, run_sim = TRUE All done!

title: Simulations of Lumpy Skin Disease Epidemics
text: lsdsim package started around Christmas time provide platform testing different responses LSD epidemics, wake mass culling imposed France led protests social unrest. nutshell: works, main features unit-tested, still development. stage: unit-testing covers core features including epidemic spread interventions; full test coverage ensured later documentation largely lacking, underway CRAN checks passing (mostly due lack documentation) package install current R versions list core functions package provided continuous integration implemented via github actions dedicated website provided using pkgdown

what: Function to simulate an LSD outbreak in a metapopulation — lsdsim
title: Function to simulate an LSD outbreak in a metapopulation — lsdsim
text: function simulates outbreak Lumpy skin disease meta-population arranged regular grid size grid_size, diffusion process defined delta matrix.

title: Function to simulate an LSD outbreak in a metapopulation — lsdsim
code: lsdsim(   grid_size = 1,   time = 365,   beta_I = 0.1,   beta_A = 0.01,   sigma = 1/7,   gamma = 1/20,   cfr = 0.1,   pasymp = 0.5,   mass_culling = FALSE,   select_culling = FALSE,   vaccination = FALSE,   quarantine = FALSE,   insecticide = FALSE,   rate_cull = 1e+30,   vacc_coverage = 0,   vacc_efficacy = 0.65,   quarant_efficacy_in = 0.2,   quarant_efficacy_out = 0.9,   insect_efficacy = 0.5,   interv_delay = 1e+30,   interv_release = 28,   delta = NULL,   diffusion = 0,   ini_S = 0,   ini_E = 0,   ini_A = 0,   ini_I = 0,   ini_C = 0,   ini_D = 0,   ini_R = 0,   ini_V = 0 )

title: Function to simulate an LSD outbreak in a metapopulation — lsdsim
text: grid_size size regular grid used. number populations number squared. Defaults 1. time integer indicating duration simulation, days. Defaults 365. beta_I rate infection symptomatic cases (); defaults 0.1. beta_A rate infection asymptomatic cases (); defaults 0.01. sigma inverse latency period; defaults 1/7. gamma inverse duration illness; defaults 1/20. cfr case fatality ratio; defaults 0.1 (.e. 10%). pasymp average proportion asymptomatic cases; defaults 0.5 (.e. 50%). mass_culling logical indicating mass culling used response. farm response takes place affected. Defaults FALSE. select_culling logical indicating selective culling used response. farm response takes place affected. Defaults FALSE. vaccination logical indicating vaccination used response. farm response takes place neighbours affected. Defaults FALSE. quarantine logical indicating quarantine used response. farm response takes place neighbours affected. Defaults FALSE. insecticide logical indicating insecticide used response. farm response takes place neighbours affected. Defaults FALSE. rate_cull rate culling takes place response triggered. Defaults 1e30, .e. animals killed 1 day. vacc_coverage vaccine coverage, .e. proportion individuals getting vaccinated response triggered. Defaults 0 - vaccination. vacc_efficacy vaccine efficacy, .e. proportion vaccinated individuals effectively become immune. Defaults 0.65 (.e. 65%). quarant_efficacy_in relative reduction transmission inside farm due quarantine. Defaults 0.2 (.e. 20%). quarant_efficacy_out relative reduction transmission towards neighbouring farms due quarantine. Defaults 0.9 (.e. 90%). insect_efficacy relative reduction transmission due insecticide spray. Defaults  0.5 (.e. 50%). interv_delay delay intervention, defined number days first symptomatic case intervention start. Defaults 1e30, .e. intervention. interv_release number days last symptomatic case intervention stops. Defaults 28 days. delta connectivity matrix defining proportions forces infection going populations (rows), populations (columns). Defaults NULL, case matrix generated automatically using rook connectivity diffusion argument. diffusion proportion force infection directed towards neighbouring populations, used build delta matrix set NULL. Defaults 0, .e. connectivity (delta identity matrix). ini_S initial number susceptible individuals, recycled needed match total number populations. Defaults 0. ini_E initial number exposed individuals, recycled needed match total number populations. Defaults 0. ini_A initial number asymptomatic cases, recycled needed match total number populations. Defaults 0. ini_I initial number symptomatic cases, recycled needed match total number populations. Defaults 0. ini_C initial number culled animals, recycled needed match total number populations. Defaults 0. ini_D initial number individuals died disease, recycled needed match total number populations. Defaults 0. ini_R initial number recovered individuals, recycled needed match total number populations. Defaults 0. ini_V initial number individuals immunized vaccination, recycled needed match total number populations. Defaults 0.

title: Function to simulate an LSD outbreak in a metapopulation — lsdsim
text: Thibaut Jombart thibautjombart@gmail.com

what: Draw LSD basic reproduction numbers — rlsd_R0_I
title: Draw LSD basic reproduction numbers — rlsd_R0_I
text: random number generator draws average values basic reproduction number (R0) LSD using literature-driven parameters log-normal distributions. rlsd_R0_I() symptomatic individuals, rlsd_R0_A asymptomatic cases. R0 defined average number secondary infections per infected cattle fully susceptible herd. Note value returned associated variability refer population average, reflect individual-level variation. values used refer transmission via vector Stomoxys calcitrans (see Table 4 source vectors), based virus isolation cattle blood.

title: Draw LSD basic reproduction numbers — rlsd_R0_I
code: rlsd_R0_I(n, mu = 20, sd = 0.02)  rlsd_R0_A(n, mu = 2, sd = 0.1)

title: Draw LSD basic reproduction numbers — rlsd_R0_I
text: Sanz-Bernardo B, Haga IR, Wijesiriwardana N, Basu S, Larner W, Diaz AV, et al. Quantifying modeling acquisition retention lumpy skin disease virus hematophagus insects reveals clinically subclinically affected cattle promoters viral transmission key targets control disease outbreaks. J Virol. 2021;95. doi:10.1128/JVI.02239-20

title: Draw LSD basic reproduction numbers — rlsd_R0_I
text: n number values draw mu population average, defaults 9.7 symptomatic individuals, 1.2 asymptomatic cases sd standard deviation lognormal distribution, defaults 0.1 symptomatic individuals, 0.1 asymptomatic cases

title: Draw LSD basic reproduction numbers — rlsd_R0_I
text: Thibaut Jombart thibautjombart@gmail.com

title: Draw LSD basic reproduction numbers — rlsd_R0_I
code: opar <- par(no.readonly = TRUE) par(mfrow = c(2, 1)) hist(rlsd_R0_I(1e5), main = "R0 for symptomatic cases", xlab = "R0") hist(rlsd_R0_A(1e5), main = "R0 for asymptomatic cases", xlab = "R0")  par(opar)

what: Draw LSD case fatality ratio — rlsd_cfr
title: Draw LSD case fatality ratio — rlsd_cfr
text: random number generator draws average case fatality ratio (CFR) LSD using literature-driven parameters beta distributions. CFR defined proportion infected cattles die disease. Reported values refer virus isolation cattle blood. Defaults correpond CFR 3%. See rbeta() details means variances.

title: Draw LSD case fatality ratio — rlsd_cfr
Draw LSD case fatality ratio — rlsd_cfr
text: Manić M, Stojiljković M, Petrović M, Nišavić J, Bacić D, Petrović T, et al. Epizootic features control measures lumpy skin disease south-east Serbia 2016. Transbound Emerg Dis. 2019;66: 2087–2099. Abera Z, Degefu H, Gari G, Dibessa ZA. Review epidemiology economic importance lumpy skin disease. Int J Basic Appl Virol. 2015. Available: https://www.academia.edu/download/76656747/2.pdf Şevik M, Doğan M. Epidemiological molecular studies lumpy skin disease outbreaks Turkey 2014-2015. Transbound Emerg Dis. 2017;64: 1268–1279.

title: Draw LSD case fatality ratio — rlsd_cfr
text: n number values draw mu mean distribution; defaults 0.03 sd standard deviation distribution; defaults 0.05

title: Draw LSD case fatality ratio — rlsd_cfr
text: Thibaut Jombart thibautjombart@gmail.com

title: Draw LSD case fatality ratio — rlsd_cfr
code: hist(rlsd_cfr(1e5), main = "CFR of LSD", xlab = "Proportion of deaths")

title: Draw LSD infectious period — rlsd_infec_period
text: random number generator draws average infectious period LSD using literature-driven parameters log-normal distributions. infectious period defined time interval onset infectiousness infected cattle recovery death. Reported values refer virus isolation cattle blood. Note value returned associated variability population average, reflect individual-level variation.

what: Draw simulation parameters for LSD — rlsd_param
title: Draw simulation parameters for LSD — rlsd_param
text: function creates tables input parameters LSD transmission using estimates Gubbins et al. (2019), based virus isolation cattle blood, assuming transmission via Stomoxys calcitrans. uses sub-functions rlsd_latent, rlsd_infec_period, rlsd_R0 rlsd_cfr. See functions change default values. Note value returned associated variability refer population average, reflect individual-level variation.

title: Draw simulation parameters for LSD — rlsd_param
Draw simulation parameters for LSD — rlsd_param
